title: some/none: evaluator
analysis: Task 163 adds evaluation support for some(expr) and none option expressions. The CompiledExprKind::OptionSome(Box<CompiledExpr>) and OptionNone AST nodes were defined in reify-types (task 162 dependency). The evaluator in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs needs two match arms in eval_expr: (1) OptionNone maps directly to Value::Option(None), and (2) OptionSome(inner) recursively evaluates the inner expression then wraps in Value::Option(Some(Box::new(val))). Critically, some() must NOT propagate Undef — some(undef) must produce Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef))), preserving the three-way distinction between Value::Option(None) (intentional absence), Value::Option(Some(Value::Undef)) (present but undefined), and Value::Undef (evaluation failure). The implementation is minimal (6 lines of evaluator code at lines 222-227) since the AST, type system, compiler, and parser are all handled by task 162. Tests in option_eval_tests.rs cover: none semantics (2 tests), some wrapping literals of int/bool/real types (3 tests), undef non-propagation with three-way inequality checks (3 tests), and nesting/value-ref integration including some(some(x)), some(none), and missing-ref wrapping (4 tests). Both implementation and all 12 tests are already present in this worktree and passing.

steps.description: Write failing tests for OptionNone evaluation in crates/reify-expr/tests/option_eval_tests.rs: (a) eval_none_produces_option_none — construct CompiledExpr::option_none(Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Int))), evaluate with empty ValueMap, assert result equals Value::Option(None). (b) eval_none_is_not_undef — same setup, assert result does NOT equal Value::Undef. These test the simplest evaluator case with no inner expression.
Add match arm in eval_expr (crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs) for CompiledExprKind::OptionNone => Value::Option(None). Single line addition after the MethodCall arm.

Write failing tests for OptionSome wrapping literal values: (a) eval_some_wraps_int — some(5) → Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Int(5)))), (b) eval_some_wraps_bool — some(true) → wrapped Bool, (c) eval_some_wraps_real — some(3.14) → wrapped Real. Each constructs CompiledExpr::option_some(CompiledExpr::literal(val, ty), Option<ty>) and evaluates with empty ValueMap.

Add match arm in eval_expr for CompiledExprKind::OptionSome(inner): recursively eval_expr(inner, ctx), wrap result in Value::Option(Some(Box::new(val))). Three lines in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs. Importantly, do NOT add any Undef short-circuiting — the inner value must be wrapped unconditionally.

Write tests validating undef non-propagation semantics (should pass immediately given step-4 implementation): (a) eval_some_undef_produces_option_some_undef — some(undef) must equal Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Undef))), (b) eval_some_undef_not_equal_to_none — Some(Undef) ≠ Option(None), (c) eval_some_undef_not_equal_to_bare_undef — Some(Undef) ≠ Value::Undef. These enforce the critical three-way distinction.

Write tests for nesting and value-ref integration (should pass given step-4): (a) eval_some_nested_some — some(some(3)) yields doubly-wrapped Option(Some(Option(Some(Int(3))))), (b) eval_some_wraps_none — some(none) yields Option(Some(Option(None))), (c) eval_some_value_ref — some(value_ref(x)) where x=42 in ValueMap yields Option(Some(Int(42))), (d) eval_some_missing_ref_wraps_undef — some(value_ref(x)) where x absent yields Option(Some(Undef)), NOT bare Undef. These validate composition and recursive evaluation correctness.

design_decisions.decision: No Undef propagation in some() — evaluate inner expression unconditionally and wrap the result
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly requires some(undef) to produce Value::Option(Some(Value::Undef)), distinct from both Value::Option(None) and Value::Undef. Unlike arithmetic operators which propagate Undef (e.g., undef + 1 → Undef), some() is a wrapper that intentionally preserves whatever the inner expression produces. This maintains the three-way distinction fundamental to option type semantics.

design_decisions.decision: Step-5 and step-6 tests are test-only steps with no corresponding impl step
design_decisions.rationale: The OptionSome implementation from step-4 is naturally correct for all cases because it unconditionally wraps eval(inner). The undef, nesting, and value-ref tests pass without additional code — they validate emergent correctness from the recursive evaluation design. Separating them as distinct test steps documents the design intent and catches future regressions if someone incorrectly adds Undef short-circuiting.

reuse.how: Used in tests to construct AST nodes. Defined as part of task 162. option_some(inner, result_type) wraps an inner CompiledExpr; option_none(result_type) creates a bare None.
Used in tests to create inner literal expressions for some() wrapping. Standard pattern across all evaluator tests.
Used in step-6 tests for value-ref integration — creates a ValueRef expression that looks up a ValueCellId in the ValueMap.
Standard test harness pattern used by all evaluator tests: create ValueMap, optionally insert values, evaluate with EvalContext::simple(&values).
OptionSome arm follows the same recursive eval pattern used by UnaryOp, BinOp, If, and other compound expression kinds.
